name: Fallout: New Vegas Action Book
description: Abstract, allowlisted NPC and GM actions for a Fallout: New Vegas bridge. These entries intentionally separate model-facing action ids from raw GECK/NVSE/xNVSE/JIP/JohnnyGuitar/ShowOff implementation notes.

4.comment: Follow a target with an AI package
4.content: Request a loaded NPC to follow or escort the player with a temporary AI package only, without registering them as a player teammate or companion.
preconditions: actor is loaded and mapped
actor is within the native 30m Gamestate radius or already cached by the bridge
runtime can issue a follow behavior for this actor
effects: actor begins following the player
actor updates their active AI behavior
examplesWhenToUse: The NPC agrees to follow the player.
A guard escorts the player to a marker.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The actor is in combat.
The requested package is not preauthored.
4.vectorizableText: npc follows player travel together accompany player come with player walk with player

execution.script:
ref rActor
ref rPlayer
float fIssued

Begin Function { rActor, rPlayer }
    let fIssued := 0
    if rActor && rPlayer
        rActor.AddScriptPackage DefaultFollowPlayerFar
        rActor.EvaluatePackage
        let fIssued := 1
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

vectorSearchTexts: can you come over here with me
stick with me for a minute
trail behind me for a bit
I need you to come with me
come on Pete I need you with me
come on Pete, I need you with me
I want to show you something come with me
I want to show you something, come with me
No I promise it is no trouble come with me there is something I really want to show you
No, I promise it is no trouble. Come with me, there is something I really want to show you.
It is safe I just want you to come with me so I can show you something
It is safe, I just want you to come with me so I can show you something.
lead the way I will follow you

6.content: Request a loaded mapped NPC to stop following the player through the native bridge.
preconditions: actor is loaded and mapped
actor is within the native 30m Gamestate radius or already cached by the bridge
actor was previously asked to follow or is currently on the bridge follow package
effects: actor stops following the player
actor returns to their normal AI behavior

execution.script:
ref rActor
float fIssued

Begin Function { rActor }
    let fIssued := 0
    if rActor
        rActor.RemoveScriptPackage
        rActor.SetPlayerTeammate 0
        let fIssued := 1
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

examplesWhenToUse: The player asks Todd/admin to make a named mapped NPC stop following the player.
The player dismisses a test follower created by the bridge.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The actor is not present in Gamestate or bridge cache.
The request is to stop attacking, stop talking, or stop an unrelated behavior.
6.vectorizableText: npc stops following player dismiss follower leave player stop accompanying player

11.content: Allow an NPC to become hostile and start combat with the player or another actor when the scene clearly warrants it.
preconditions: actor is loaded and mapped
actor is within the native 30m Gamestate radius or already cached by the bridge
bridge policy allows combat
effects: actor starts combat with the player
examplesWhenToUse: The player threatens or attacks the NPC.
A hostile creature notices the player.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The NPC is only annoyed.
Faction rules or quest state should prevent combat.
11.vectorizableText: npc attacks target starts combat becomes hostile fights someone

execution.script:
ref rActor
ref rTarget
float fIssued

Begin Function { rActor, rTarget }
    let fIssued := 0
    if rActor && rTarget
        rActor.StartCombat rTarget
        let fIssued := 1
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

12.content: Request a loaded mapped NPC to stop fighting the player through the native bridge.
preconditions: actor is loaded and mapped
actor is within the native 30m Gamestate radius or already cached by the bridge
actor is currently fighting or being asked to stand down
effects: actor stops combat
hostile alarm against the player is cleared when possible

execution.script:
ref rActor
ref rTarget
float fIssued

Begin Function { rActor, rTarget }
    let fIssued := 0
    if rActor
        rActor.StopCombat
        if rTarget
            rTarget.StopCombatAlarmOnActor
        endif
        let fIssued := 1
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

examplesWhenToUse: Todd/admin asks a named nearby mapped NPC to stop attacking or fighting the player.
The player asks a hostile test NPC to stand down.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The actor is not present in Gamestate or bridge cache.
The request is about stopping follow/sandbox behavior rather than combat.
12.vectorizableText: npc stops combat ends fight stands down calms down ceases fire

13.comment: Wait or stay at current position
13.content: Request a loaded mapped NPC to stop following and hold near their current position with the native sandbox no-move package.
preconditions: actor is loaded and mapped
actor is within the native 30m Gamestate radius or already cached by the bridge
effects: actor stops bridge follow behavior
actor receives a no-move sandbox package near their current position

execution.script:
ref rActor
float fIssued

Begin Function { rActor }
    let fIssued := 0
    if rActor
        rActor.SetPlayerTeammate 0
        rActor.AddScriptPackage DefaultSandboxNoMoveCurrentLocation200
        rActor.EvaluatePackage
        let fIssued := 1
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

examplesWhenToUse: Todd/admin asks a named nearby mapped NPC to wait here, stay put, or hold position.
The player wants a test NPC to stop moving around.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The request is to follow the player.
The request is to relax, sit, or wander around nearby furniture.
13.vectorizableText: npc waits at current place stays here holds position remains still

14.content: Request a loaded mapped NPC to sandbox around their current position, allowing the engine to choose nearby idle markers, conversation, or wandering.
preconditions: actor is loaded and mapped
actor is within the native 30m Gamestate radius or already cached by the bridge
Todd/admin explicitly wants broad ambient sandbox behavior
effects: actor stops bridge follow behavior
actor receives a current-location sandbox package
actor may wander or use ambient idle markers selected by the engine

execution.script:
ref rActor
float fIssued

Begin Function { rActor }
    let fIssued := 0
    if rActor
        rActor.SetPlayerTeammate 0
        rActor.AddScriptPackage DefaultSandboxCurrentLocation256
        rActor.EvaluatePackage
        let fIssued := 1
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

examplesWhenToUse: Todd/admin asks a named nearby mapped NPC to sandbox, hang around, wander nearby, or act natural.
The player wants an NPC to use broad nearby ambient idle markers.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The request is specifically to sit down or take a seat.
The request requires a guaranteed specific furniture reference.
The request is to freeze in place without wandering.
14.vectorizableText: npc idles nearby wanders locally relaxes around current place acts natural

15.content: Request a loaded mapped NPC to clear their bridge-added script package and return to their regular package stack.
preconditions: actor is loaded and mapped
actor is within the native 30m Gamestate radius or already cached by the bridge
actor was previously given a bridge script package or should return to regular AI
effects: actor's current bridge script package is removed
actor is no longer marked as a player teammate by the bridge

execution.script:
ref rActor
float fIssued

Begin Function { rActor }
    let fIssued := 0
    if rActor
        rActor.RemoveScriptPackage
        rActor.SetPlayerTeammate 0
        let fIssued := 1
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

examplesWhenToUse: Todd/admin asks a named nearby mapped NPC to go back to normal or stop their current bridge behavior.
The player wants to clear wait/sandbox/follow behavior from a test NPC.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The request is to stop combat only.
The request is to start a new follow, wait, or sandbox behavior.
15.vectorizableText: npc resumes normal routine clears temporary behavior returns to default schedule
vectorSearchTexts: go back to what you were doing

16.comment: Sit down or use nearby furniture
16.content: Request a loaded mapped NPC to take a seat or use nearby valid furniture through the native current-location AI package.
preconditions: actor is loaded and mapped
actor is within the native 30m Gamestate radius or already cached by the bridge
nearby valid furniture exists if the user expects an actual sitting animation
effects: actor stops bridge follow behavior
actor receives a current-location package that can select valid nearby furniture
actor may sit when the engine finds a usable seat

execution.script:
ref rActor
float fIssued

Begin Function { rActor }
    let fIssued := 0
    if rActor
        rActor.SetPlayerTeammate 0
        rActor.AddScriptPackage DefaultSandboxCurrentLocation256
        rActor.EvaluatePackage
        let fIssued := 1
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

examplesWhenToUse: A nearby NPC is asked to sit down, take a seat, sit here, or use a chair.
The player wants a normal NPC to try nearby valid furniture without granting broad sandbox/admin access.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The request is to wander, sandbox, or resume the actor's normal routine.
The request requires a guaranteed specific furniture reference.
16.vectorizableText: npc sits down uses nearby chair bench furniture takes a seat

17.pluginSource: vanilla GECK idle animation via native bridge allowlist
preconditions: actor is loaded and mapped
actor can play standing idle animations
effects: actor plays the vanilla LooseWaveHello idle gesture

execution.script:
ref rActor
float fIssued

Begin Function { rActor }
    let fIssued := 0
    if rActor
        rActor.PlayIdle LooseWaveHello
        let fIssued := 1
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

examplesWhenToUse: The player asks an NPC to wave, wave hello, greet someone, or say hi with a gesture.
Todd/admin asks a named nearby mapped NPC to wave.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The request is only normal spoken dialogue without a requested greeting gesture.
The actor is in combat or should keep performing a higher priority behavior.

20.comment: Spawn an item at a player or nearby NPC anchor
20.content: Request the bridge to place one catalog-selected item at the player by default, or at a named nearby NPC when spawnTarget names one. The placement anchor must be the player/me, a nearby named person, or an explicit known place anchor from the current context; do not invent arbitrary spawn locations. If the user's dictated target sounds close to a nearby person's name, use that nearby person's visible name as spawnTarget.
20.pluginSource: xNVSE native bridge trusted form allowlist

parametersSchema.catalogItemId: one candidate id from scoped catalog fallout-new-vegas.items
parametersSchema.spawnTarget: optional placement anchor: player, me, or the visible name/key/ref of a nearby NPC/person in the current gamestate; prefer visible nearby names over invented locations when the user's wording is ambiguous

preconditions: player is loaded
requested item appears in the scoped catalog candidates
bridge can resolve the catalog item to a trusted base-game form
effects: one or more copies of the selected item are placed at the chosen player or nearby NPC anchor

execution.script:
ref rTarget
ref rItem
int iCount
int iSpawned
float fIssued

Begin Function { rTarget, rItem, iCount }
    let fIssued := 0
    let iSpawned := 0
    if iCount < 1
        let iCount := 1
    endif
    if rTarget && rItem
        while iSpawned < iCount
            rTarget.PlaceAtMe rItem 1
            let iSpawned := iSpawned + 1
        loop
        let fIssued := iSpawned
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

examplesWhenToUse: Todd/admin is asked to spawn, create, place, or give a specific catalog item.
The player asks for a test item to appear in the world.
The request names a nearby NPC or the player as the placement anchor, such as at Sunny, on Pete, or at my feet.
The dictated target is close to a nearby visible name, such as Easy Peak meaning Easy Pete.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The request only mentions an item without asking to spawn, create, place, or give it.
The requested item is not among scoped catalog candidates.
20.vectorizableText: create item spawn item place object give item near player nearby npc anchor

scopedCatalogs.title: Spawnable item candidates
scopedCatalogs.description: Searches item names, aliases, tags, and vector text only after a spawn-style request.

vectorSearchTexts: put an item at my feet

21.comment: Spawn an NPC or creature at a player or nearby NPC anchor
21.content: Request the bridge to place one catalog-selected NPC or creature base form at the player by default, or at a named nearby NPC when spawnTarget names one. The placement anchor must be the player/me, a nearby named person, or an explicit known place anchor from the current context; do not invent arbitrary spawn locations. If the user's dictated target sounds close to a nearby person's name, use that nearby person's visible name as spawnTarget.
21.pluginSource: xNVSE native bridge trusted form allowlist

parametersSchema.catalogEntityId: one candidate id from scoped catalog fallout-new-vegas.entities
parametersSchema.spawnTarget: optional placement anchor: player, me, or the visible name/key/ref of a nearby NPC/person in the current gamestate; prefer visible nearby names over invented locations when the user's wording is ambiguous

preconditions: player is loaded
requested NPC or creature appears in the scoped catalog candidates
bridge can resolve the catalog entity to a trusted base-game form
effects: one or more copies of the selected NPC or creature are placed at the chosen player or nearby NPC anchor

execution.script:
ref rTarget
ref rEntity
int iCount
int iSpawned
float fIssued

Begin Function { rTarget, rEntity, iCount }
    let fIssued := 0
    let iSpawned := 0
    if iCount < 1
        let iCount := 1
    endif
    if rTarget && rEntity
        while iSpawned < iCount
            rTarget.PlaceAtMe rEntity 1
            let iSpawned := iSpawned + 1
        loop
        let fIssued := iSpawned
    endif
    SetFunctionValue fIssued
End

examplesWhenToUse: Todd/admin is asked to spawn, create, place, or summon a specific NPC, creature, actor, or entity.
The player asks for a test enemy, friendly NPC, or creature to appear in the world.
The request names a nearby NPC or the player as the placement anchor, such as spawn a deathclaw on Pete or summon a gecko at Sunny.
The dictated target is close to a nearby visible name, such as Easy Peak meaning Easy Pete.
examplesWhenNotToUse: The request is to spawn an item, weapon, aid, ammo, note, or miscellaneous object instead of a living actor or creature.
The requested entity is not among scoped catalog candidates.
21.vectorizableText: create actor spawn npc spawn creature summon living entity near player nearby npc anchor

scopedCatalogs.title: Spawnable NPC and creature candidates
scopedCatalogs.description: Searches NPC_ and CREA names, aliases, tags, and vector text only after a spawn-style request.

vectorSearchTexts: summon an enemy near the player
